description: Replace the unreliable proactive extension reload mechanism with a content-hash-based approach. A post-build script computes a SHA256 hash of the extension bundle files and embeds it as window.__EXTENSION_HASH__ in the side panel bundle via esbuild banner. The server reads this hash from the installed side panel JS and sends it in sync.full. The side panel compares the server's hash against its own frozen-in-memory hash and shows a retro-themed dialog prompting the user to reload when a mismatch is detected. This works in both production (version bump) and dev mode (any rebuild). Spike-tested: Chrome strips custom manifest.json fields, but window globals set via esbuild banner ARE accessible and stay frozen in memory after disk changes.

userStories.title: Compute extension content hash at build time and embed in side panel bundle
userStories.description: Add a post-build step that computes a SHA256 hash of the key extension bundle files and embeds it as window.__EXTENSION_HASH__ in the side panel bundle. Also write the hash to a .extension-hash file for the server to read.
acceptanceCriteria: After npm run build, dist/side-panel/side-panel.js starts with a window.__EXTENSION_HASH__ assignment
A .extension-hash file exists in platform/browser-extension/ containing the same 16-char hex hash
The hash is computed from the concatenated contents of dist/background.js, dist/side-panel/side-panel.js (pre-hash), dist/side-panel/styles.css, and dist/offscreen/index.js
Two consecutive builds with identical source produce the same hash
Changing any source file produces a different hash after rebuild

userStories.title: Send extensionHash in sync.full from the MCP server
userStories.description: The MCP server reads the extensionHash from the .extension-hash file in the installed extension directory and includes it in the sync.full payload and ConfigStateResult so the side panel can compare it against its own hash.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStateResult in platform/shared/src/index.ts has an extensionHash optional string field
buildConfigStatePayload reads the hash from ~/.opentabs/extension/.extension-hash and includes it in the payload
sync.full params include extensionHash
The side panel receives extensionHash via getFullState and stores it in state
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (add extensionHash to ConfigStateResult interface)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (read .extension-hash file, include in payload)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (add extensionHash to FullStateResult)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (add extensionHash state variable)
- platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts (add extensionHash to cache interface)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (extract extensionHash in handleSyncFull)
- platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts (return extensionHash in getFullState)

Context:
The serverVersion field already flows through this exact pipeline. extensionHash follows the same path.

In platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts, buildConfigStatePayload (lines 346-397):
- Import `readFileSync` and `join` from node builtins
- Import `getExtensionDir` from `./config.js`
- Read the hash:
```typescript
let extensionHash: string | undefined;
try {
  const hashPath = join(getExtensionDir(), '.extension-hash');
  extensionHash = readFileSync(hashPath, 'utf-8').trim() || undefined;
} catch {
  // Hash file not present — skip
}
```
- Add `extensionHash,` to the returned object alongside `serverVersion` at line 393

For the rest of the pipeline, follow the exact same pattern as serverVersion:

1. ConfigStateResult (shared/src/index.ts line ~381): add `extensionHash?: string;`

2. server-state-cache.ts: add `extensionHash?: string;` to ServerStateCache interface

3. message-router.ts handleSyncFull (around line ~463): extract:
```typescript
const rawExtensionHash = typeof params.extensionHash === 'string' ? params.extensionHash : undefined;
```
Include in updateServerStateCache call.

4. background-message-handlers.ts handleBgGetFullState (around line ~258): add:
```typescript
extensionHash: serverCache.extensionHash,
```

5. bridge.ts FullStateResult: add `extensionHash?: string;`

6. App.tsx:
- Add state: `const [extensionHash, setExtensionHash] = useState<string | undefined>(undefined);`
- In applyFullState: `setExtensionHash(result.extensionHash);`
- On disconnect (line ~335): `setExtensionHash(undefined);`

Constraints:
- Read the hash synchronously (readFileSync) since buildConfigStatePayload should be fast.
- If the file doesn't exist, return undefined — don't fail.
- Follow the exact same pattern as serverVersion in every file.

userStories.title: Add ExtensionUpdateDialog component with retro-themed reload prompt
userStories.description: Create a new side panel component that shows a blocking dialog when the extension hash from the server doesn't match the running extension's frozen hash (window.__EXTENSION_HASH__). The dialog uses the retro design system and has a Reload button.
acceptanceCriteria: ExtensionUpdateDialog renders a retro Dialog when server extensionHash differs from window.__EXTENSION_HASH__
Dialog has a yellow header bar reading 'Extension Updated' with no close X button
Dialog body text explains the extension has been updated and needs to reload
Dialog has a single 'Reload' button with a RefreshCw icon that calls chrome.runtime.reload()
Dialog cannot be dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape
Dialog does not appear when extensionHash is undefined or when hashes match
Dialog does not appear when not connected to the server
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ExtensionUpdateDialog.tsx (new file) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (render the dialog, compute mismatch) Context: The retro Dialog component is at `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/Dialog.tsx`. Reference the unreviewed plugin confirmation dialog in PluginCard.tsx (lines 343-367) for the pattern. The design system uses: - `font-head` (Archivo Black) for headers - `font-sans` (Space Grotesk) for body text - `bg-primary` (#ffdb33) for dialog header bar - `text-foreground` for body text - `text-muted-foreground` for secondary text - Retro Button component at `components/retro/Button` - RefreshCw icon from lucide-react (already used elsewhere) IMPORTANT: The retro Dialog.Header (retro/Dialog.tsx lines 43-52) includes a built-in X close button. This dialog must NOT be closable. Do NOT use Dialog.Header. Instead, create a custom header div with the same styling but no X button: ```tsx
Extension Updated
``` Reading the side panel's own hash: ```typescript const runningHash: string | undefined = (window as unknown as Record).__EXTENSION_HASH__ as string | undefined; ``` In App.tsx, compute mismatch: ```typescript const runningHash = (window as unknown as Record).__EXTENSION_HASH__ as string | undefined; const showUpdateDialog = connected && extensionHash !== undefined && runningHash !== undefined && extensionHash !== runningHash; ``` Render at the top level of App return (overlays everything): ```tsx ``` The dialog component: ```tsx import { RefreshCw } from 'lucide-react'; import { Dialog } from './retro/Dialog'; import { Button } from './retro/Button'; interface ExtensionUpdateDialogProps { open: boolean; } const ExtensionUpdateDialog = ({ open }: ExtensionUpdateDialogProps) => ( {}}> e.preventDefault()} onEscapeKeyDown={(e: Event) => e.preventDefault()} > {/* Custom header without X button */}
Extension Updated

A new version of the extension has been installed. Reload to apply the update.

); export { ExtensionUpdateDialog }; ``` Constraints: - Must follow the retro design system exactly — same border, shadow, colors. - Must NOT be dismissable. - `onOpenChange={() => {}}` prevents Radix from closing the dialog. - The dialog should feel polished, not alarming — it's a routine update, not an error.

userStories.title: Remove proactive version-change extension reload paths
userStories.description: Remove the pendingExtensionReload flag and the version-change-triggered sendExtensionReload calls from the MCP server. The side panel hash check replaces this mechanism. Keep the auth secret rotation reload and the extension_reload MCP tool.
acceptanceCriteria: pendingExtensionReload field is removed from ServerState interface and createState
The version-change block in performReload (reload.ts lines 480-497) no longer calls sendExtensionReload or sets pendingExtensionReload
The deferred reload check in http-routes.ts (lines 1067-1077) is removed
The auth secret rotation reload in reload.ts (lines 402-414) is preserved unchanged
The extension_reload MCP tool in browser-tools/reload-extension.ts is preserved unchanged
sendExtensionReload function is preserved (still used by auth secret rotation)

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for extension hash mismatch detection and reload dialog
userStories.description: Add comprehensive E2E tests covering the extension hash mismatch detection, the reload dialog UI, dev mode rebuild scenario, and stress tests for rapid reconnection cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test verifies the ExtensionUpdateDialog appears when server sends a different extensionHash than what the running extension has
E2E test verifies the dialog does NOT appear when hashes match
E2E test verifies the dialog cannot be dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape
E2E test verifies the Reload button is present and clickable
E2E test simulates a dev mode rebuild: modifies .extension-hash file, triggers hot reload, dialog appears
Stress test: rapid connect/disconnect cycles with matching hashes do not cause spurious dialog appearances or crashes
E2E test verifies the old proactive reload path no longer sends extension.reload on version mismatch
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-extension-update.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
The spike test confirmed the approach works. Key findings:
- `launchExtensionContext` copies extension from `platform/browser-extension/` (NOT ~/.opentabs/extension/). Source: fixtures.ts line 67 `const EXTENSION_DIR = path.join(ROOT, 'platform/browser-extension');` and line 857 `fs.cpSync(EXTENSION_DIR, extensionDir, ...)`.
- `window.__EXTENSION_HASH__` is accessible from sidePanelPage.evaluate() and stays frozen after disk changes.
- The server reads .extension-hash from the configDir's extension/ subdirectory.

Approach for simulating hash mismatch:

**Test 1: Dialog appears on hash mismatch**
1. Start server normally (configDir has extension with hash A from build)
2. The side panel bundle has hash A embedded as window.__EXTENSION_HASH__
3. Write a different hash B to configDir's extension/.extension-hash
4. Trigger hot reload (SIGUSR1) or POST /reload so server re-reads the hash
5. Server sends hash B in sync.full, side panel has hash A → mismatch → dialog

To set this up:
- After `startMcpServer` and `launchExtensionContext`, the hashes match (both from the same build)
- Overwrite `path.join(configDir, 'extension', '.extension-hash')` with a fake hash
- Trigger server reload: `server.triggerHotReload()` for hot-reload mode or `POST /reload`
- Wait for side panel to receive the updated sync.full
- Check for dialog: `sidePanelPage.getByText('Extension Updated')`

Wait — the server reads the hash from the installed extension dir in the configDir. But the test uses hot-reload mode (dev proxy). The hash is read in `buildConfigStatePayload` which is called on every `sendSyncFull`. So after hot reload → new sendSyncFull → reads new hash → sends to extension → side panel compares → dialog.

But actually, buildConfigStatePayload reads the hash on every call (readFileSync), so even without a hot reload, the next `plugins.changed` event would trigger `getFullState()` which returns the cached serverState which was set during the last sync.full. We need a hot reload or explicit sync.full trigger.

Simplest approach: use hot reload mode (default in fixtures). After writing the fake hash, call `server.triggerHotReload()`. The worker restarts, calls sendSyncFull with the new hash.

**Test 2: Dialog does NOT appear when hashes match**
- Normal startup, no modifications
- Open side panel, verify `sidePanelPage.getByText('Extension Updated')` is NOT visible

**Test 3: Dialog cannot be dismissed**
- Simulate mismatch (same as test 1)
- Verify dialog visible
- Click outside: `sidePanelPage.locator('body').click({ position: { x: 5, y: 5 } })` (outside dialog)
- Verify dialog still visible
- Press Escape: `sidePanelPage.keyboard.press('Escape')`
- Verify dialog still visible

**Test 4: Dev mode rebuild simulation**
- Start with hot reload
- Modify .extension-hash in configDir extension dir
- Trigger hot reload
- Verify dialog appears
(This is essentially the same as test 1 but framed as dev mode)

**Stress test: Rapid reconnections**
- Start normally (hashes match)
- Open side panel
- Rapidly disconnect/reconnect the extension (simulate by killing and re-creating WebSocket or by calling extension_reload multiple times)
- Actually, for stress: call `server.triggerHotReload()` multiple times in quick succession
- Verify no dialog appears (hashes still match since we didn't change .extension-hash)
- Verify no page errors

**Test: Proactive reload removed**
- Write a stale .opentabs-version to configDir
- Start server (ensureExtensionInstalled detects version mismatch, copies files)
- Verify server logs contain 'side panel will detect hash change'
- Verify server logs do NOT contain 'sending reload signal'

Key selectors:
- Dialog: `sidePanelPage.getByText('Extension Updated')`
- Reload button: `sidePanelPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Reload' })`
- Dialog role: `sidePanelPage.locator('[role=dialog]')`

Constraints:
- Follow same setup/teardown pattern as existing side panel E2E tests
- Use `test.slow()` for tests involving hot reload
- Clean up resources in finally blocks
- Use `waitForExtensionConnected(server)` before opening side panel
- Use `waitForLog(server, ...)` for server-side assertions
- Note that fixtures.ts line 515-522 pre-writes .opentabs-version to prevent version mismatch reload. This is still needed for non-hash-related tests. But for the 'proactive reload removed' test, you may need to NOT pre-write it, or write a different version.
- For the hash mismatch test, you need to first verify what hash the side panel has. You can read it via `sidePanelPage.evaluate(() => (window as any).__EXTENSION_HASH__)`. Then write a DIFFERENT hash to the server's .extension-hash file.
